child_geo_events.address: 300 E Green St, Pasadena, CA 91101, USA
child_geo_events.name: Evacuation Shelter - Pasadena Convention Center

data.information:
An evacuation shelter is available at the Pasadena Convention Center, 300 East Green Street, Pasadena, CA 91101 per the LA County website.

Parking is available at the parking structure across the street from the Convention Center, located at 155 E. Green St. Please enter from Marengo Ave.

Parking is also available at the Paseo parking structure, located at 360 E. Colorado Blvd. You are able to enter the structure from Colorado Blvd. or Green St.

The Humane Society is on-site to accept pets for boarding. Service animals will be allowed inside the Convention Center.

Residents are able to call 211 for information regarding shelters, and social and health services.

child_geo_events.name: Family Assistance Center

data.information:
Friday, January 10: 10 a.m. to 6 p.m.
Saturday, January 11: 9 a.m. to 5 p.m.

Cheviot Hills Recreation Center
2551 Motor Avenue, Los Angeles, CA 90064, Phone (310) 837-5186

The Cheviot Hills location is operated by LA City, and Los Angeles Police officers will be present to take missing persons reports. The Mayor’s Crisis Response Team, American Red Cross, LA County Medical Examiner’s Office, and LA County Department of Mental Health will provide additional information and support. For more information on missing loved ones, please call (800) 675-5799 or visit the Red Cross Finding Friends & Family page.

child_geo_events.address: 361 S Raymond Ave, Pasadena, CA 91105, USA
child_geo_events.name: Small Animal Shelter - Pasadena Humane Society

data.information:
A small animal shelter is established at the Pasadena Humane Society for emergency sheltering. The shelter is located at 361 S Raymond Ave, Pasadena, CA 91105. The shelter states, "If you are being evacuated and can go somewhere with your pets–such as a friend or family member’s home–that is ideal. If you are evacuating, please take your animals with you. We are very thankful that the Pasadena Civic Center has allowed people to stay with their pets. We are helping in any way that we can–providing them with resources such as food and leashes for the displaced families. We also have pet food available at the shelter for those who need it. If you cannot go somewhere that allows your pet to stay with you, you can bring your animals to the shelter where we are offering emergency boarding."

More details are located at this link.

child_geo_events.address: 1605 Garfield Avenue, South Pasadena, CA
child_geo_events.name: South Pasadena San Marino YMCA Disaster Relief Services

data.information:
Anyone affected by the fires is welcome to use our facilities—our showers, our Wi-Fi, workout, and child activity center—or just come for community. We have toiletries, non-perishable food, water, clothes of all sizes, shoes, blankets, bedding, pillows, baby supplies, diapers, pet supplies, phones, and chargers.

(626) 799-9119

links.label: South Pasadena San Marino YMCA Disaster Relief Services Facebook

child_geo_events.address: 450 N Lake Avenue, Pasadena
child_geo_events.name: Bottled Water Distribution Center

data.information:
For PWP customers in the Eaton Fire-impacted areas, the City of Pasadena and LA County Public Works are opening a bottled water distribution center at 450 N. Lake Ave, today, January 10, from noon to 5 p.m. and tomorrow, January 11, from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m.

Please enter, heading north on Lake Ave. At this time, there is a limit of one case per household. We do not need additional volunteers at this time.

https://www.cityofpasadena.net/
